items.scenario: You are taking an introductory chemistry course this term. Your professor announced that the midterm has been moved to a week earlier, which overlaps with a research presentation you must give for another class.
items.direction: Write an email to your professor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the scheduling conflict you are facing
Ask whether an alternative exam time is possible
Describe how you plan to prepare for the midterm
items.subject: Conflict with the rescheduled midterm date

items.scenario: You are a second-year student hoping to apply for a teaching assistant position in the economics department next semester. You saw a notice that applications open soon but it listed few details.
items.direction: Write an email to the department coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell the coordinator why you are interested in the position
Ask what qualifications applicants need
Request information about the application deadline
items.subject: Questions about the TA application process

items.scenario: You recently submitted a draft of your senior thesis and received written comments from your advisor. Some of the feedback on the methodology section was unclear to you.
items.direction: Write an email to your advisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe which part of the feedback confused you
Ask for clarification on the methodology suggestion
Request a short meeting to discuss the next steps
items.subject: Clarifying your comments on my thesis draft

items.scenario: You signed up for a weekend pottery workshop at the campus arts center. Two days before it starts, you learned the session time was changed from morning to evening, which you cannot attend.
items.direction: Write an email to the workshop organizer. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that the new time does not work for you
Ask whether a different session is available
Request a refund if no alternative exists
items.subject: Time change for the weekend pottery workshop

items.scenario: You completed a summer internship at a small publishing firm last month. Your supervisor mentioned there might be a part-time opening during the school year, but you have not heard back since.
items.direction: Write an email to your former supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank your supervisor for the internship experience
Ask whether the part-time role is still available
Tell your supervisor about your current availability
items.subject: Following up on the part-time opportunity

items.scenario: You have lived in a university apartment for two months. The heating in your unit has been making a loud rattling noise at night, and it has started to affect your sleep before exams.
items.direction: Write an email to the housing manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the heating in your unit
Explain how the noise is affecting you
Ask when a repair can be scheduled
items.subject: Rattling noise from the heater in my apartment

items.scenario: You are part of a student photography club. The club has been meeting indoors all semester, and you think an outdoor sunset photo walk would attract more members and add variety.
items.direction: Write an email to the club president. In your email, do the following:
goals: Suggest organizing an outdoor sunset photo walk
Explain why members might enjoy the change
Ask how you can help arrange the event
items.subject: Idea for an outdoor club photo walk

items.scenario: You recently joined a campus debate society and attended your first practice tournament. The event was well run, but the room assigned was too small for the number of participants.
items.direction: Write an email to the society's coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe your impression of the practice tournament
Explain the problem caused by the small room
Suggest reserving a larger space next time
items.subject: Feedback on the practice tournament setup
